summary: This source is a brief summary of a TED-Ed animation about Hypatia of Alexandria. It outlines her role as a public intellectual, Neoplatonist philosopher, and secular advisor in the Eastern Roman Empire, noting her belief in mathematical harmony and her eventual murder by religious zealots.

principles.statement: The harmonies of mathematics serve as a window into the divine.
principles.rationale: As a leader of Alexandria's Platonic school, she embraced Neoplatonism, which viewed mathematical structure and harmony as a way to understand divine truths.
principles.source_quote: a belief system that saw the harmonies of mathematics as a window into the divine.

mental_models.name: Mathematical Harmony as a Divine Lens
mental_models.description: Viewing the structured, harmonious nature of mathematics not merely as a secular tool, but as a direct reflection of and window into the divine order of the universe (a core tenet of her Neoplatonism).
